title: verbascum rotundifolium subsp. conocarpum
intro: Verbascum rotundifolium subsp. conocarpum, also known as the round-leaved mullein, is a perennial plant species belonging to the mullein genus, characterized by its large, rounded leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which can help to soothe skin irritations, reduce swelling, and combat infections. The plant is rich in mucilages, which can provide a range of health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, Verbascum rotundifolium subsp. conocarpum can be used to make infusions, tinctures, and ointments, which can be applied topically to the skin or consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
